prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
Alright, let's get to it.

That task that's been staring at you all morning? It's just something you have to do, not something you are. So don't let it define you.

You've procrastinated enough. That feeling you get right before starting something difficult—that's not fear, it's just your brain trying to protect you from discomfort. But discomfort is where growth happens. You've overcome hard things before. Remember that.

Right now, what you feel is anticipation. That weight in your stomach isn't hopelessness; it's momentum building. Take one small step. Just open the document, just gather one material, just make the first five minutes. Once you start, the momentum will carry you. You don't need to finish it all today, you just need to start.

Set a timer for 25 minutes. Focus on nothing else during that time. No checking your phone, no side tasks. Just work. Then you can take a real break, refuel, and come back fresh. The hardest part was starting, and you're about to do it.

You can do this. Go get it done.
